_readme: FRAME OVERRIDES — local control over every frame: transitions, elements, text.
Applied LAST, wins over animation.json, audio-cues.json and the frames' own data-*.
Survives Claude Design re-exports (the SVGs are overwritten; this file is not).
Find ids: node scripts/inspect-frame.js frame_N  (see CLAUDE.md 'Tweak Any Frame').
Per element: at dur x y scale rotate opacity hide idle orbit  (text: cps type caret)
at = ABSOLUTE film seconds (same clock as data-t / cue-bindings.json).
emphasis { scale, dur, at } — a one-time punch-and-settle AFTER the element lands,
for the beat the VO stresses a word (motion-direction accent).
Per frame: _frame { x y scale } · _transition { inFrom outTo distance frames } · _note
Grounded in the ballast reference (knowledge-vault/14). mp_v5's cues already build
most scenes one-per-beat (f2/f4/f9/f15/f17/f18) — the reference is RESTRAINED, so
this pass is surgical: cascade the few CLUMPS where many elements landed on one
beat, land heroes LAST as the payoff, and punch the STAT/hero beats. Nothing here
over-animates; it makes the eye read a sentence instead of a clump.

frame_0.svg._note: COVER. Cascade the pillars, land the anchor LAST. INNER LIFE after appearing (Comments c1-c6): pillar_disc_4=helm spins slow clockwise; pillar_disc_1=gears turn; pillar_disc_2=stopwatch shakes; pillar_disc_3=shield breathes; anchor sways. (helm/wheel are single centred shapes so whole-group rotation spins them true; the multi-part gears + shield reflection + clock needle + soundwave need per-shape ids from Design to do fully.)

frame_2.svg._note: SIX KEY AREAS. Targets resolved from the reviewer's regions (resolve-comment-targets.js), since the ids are mislabelled: env_seaweed (bottom-left) is actually the FISH school -> swim (c12); the real bottom-right plant is xmlid_...876128_ -> wave-warp (c13); tile_master_pilot carries a spec bob the reviewer wants gone -> idle off (c14).

frame_1.svg._note: CONVERGENCE — build one-by-one in SETS, not all at once (Comments c8-c11), and the magnifier (card_center) lands LAST (c7). Columns/rows resolved from the review regions: Set A = left column (centre concept_icon_3 first, then top person_3 + bottom person_2); Set B = top row (concept_icon_4/5/6); Set C = bottom row (concept_icon_2/1 + centre person_1); Set D = right column (person_5/4). Timing kept inside the original ~44-47s window so VO sync holds; reviewer can nudge. Idle keeps them alive on the hold.

frame_3.svg._note: SCENARIO. Content-heavy (title + mariner + 3 dashed-ring icons + AB/Bosun pair), no visible background for the world blobs, so it read frozen. Give the two mariner groups and the helmet badge a gentle breathe, and let the gear icon spin — the reference keeps a full scene like this alive with ring/gear motion, not a dead hold.

frame_14.svg._note: SCENARIO. The container ship rocks (vessel idle), but the two engineers dominate the frame and held it static. Float the two speech bubbles (natural for a chat bubble) and let the engineers shift gently, so the exchange reads as live conversation, not a freeze-frame.

frame_6.svg._note: SCENARIO. The 4 tug/concept icons clumped on one beat (190s). Cascade them left-to-right as the reference builds a list one item at a time.

frame_10.svg._note: STAT. The '30' counts up (good, kv14 s10) but had no accent. Add the reference's big-stat punch as the number settles, and cascade the 4 orbit discs.

frame_12.svg._note: SCENARIO. 5 elements clumped at 339s. Stage them: cranes slide in, then the ship, then the bubble, then the two figures — so the set reads as assembling, not appearing.

frame_15.svg._note: CONVERGENCE. Benefit discs already build via arrow-draw→disc-pop (good). Add the reference's '30' emphasis punch on the final benefit disc as it lands.

frame_19.svg._note: CLOSE. 5 recap icons clumped onto the orbit at 560s. Cascade them so the recap reads as a sweep round the ring, then the closing keyword washes in.
